message:
chore(beads): track Slack webhook and AbortSignal subscription work

Updates issue tracking:
- bd-1pgk7: Closed - Slack webhook notification channel implemented
- bd-jb6ka: In progress - AgentDriver.subscribe abortable implementation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"bd-jb6ka","title":"Make AgentDriver.subscribe abortable to prevent leaked monitors","description":"Problem:
- handleAgentEvents() uses an AbortController but cannot cancel the underlying driver.subscribe() async iterable.
- When replacing monitors while the agent is idle (no events), the old subscription can stay registered longer than intended.

Plan:
- Extend AgentDriver.subscribe(agentId, signal?) and implement abort handling in BaseDriver.subscribe().
- Update apps/gateway/src/services/agent.ts so handleAgentEvents() creates the subscription with its AbortSignal.
- Add a focused unit test covering abort cleanup (subscriber removed without needing a terminated event).
","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-30T05:50:49.736992222Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T05:51:18.190188492Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-jb6ka","title":"Make AgentDriver.subscribe abortable to prevent leaked monitors","description":"Problem:
- handleAgentEvents() uses an AbortController but cannot cancel the underlying driver.subscribe() async iterable.
- When replacing monitors while the agent is idle (no events), the old subscription can stay registered longer than intended.

Plan:
- Extend AgentDriver.subscribe(agentId, signal?) and implement abort handling in BaseDriver.subscribe().
- Update apps/gateway/src/services/agent.ts so handleAgentEvents() creates the subscription with its AbortSignal.
- Add a focused unit test covering abort cleanup (subscriber removed without needing a terminated event).
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-30T05:50:49.736992222Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T05:56:20.528918038Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T05:56:20.528846095Z","close_reason":"Add AbortSignal support to AgentDriver.subscribe and wire into handleAgentEvents","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-jfoa","title":"Backwards compatibility + migration for /setup responses","description":"Goal:
- Ensure existing clients/UX are not broken by manifest-driven fields.

Scope:
- Preserve existing response keys; add new keys as optional.
- Document migration and fallback behavior.

Tests / Verification (detailed logging required):
- Contract: Covered by existing OpenAPI contract tests (bd-2lvp closed).
- Integration: bd-a1jg includes /setup response validation.

Definition of Done:
